Grape___Black_rot.description: A serious fungal disease that attacks all green parts of the vine.
Grape___Black_rot.symptoms:
• Small brown spots on leaves
• Fruit turns black and shrivels into mummies
• Circular lesions on shoots
Grape___Black_rot.prevention:
• Remove mummified berries
• Prune for better sunlight
• Keep area around vines clean
Grape___Black_rot.treatment: Apply fungicides like Myclobutanil or Mancozeb before bloom.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).description: A complex disease affecting the wood and leaves, often causing tiger-stripe patterns.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).symptoms:
• Interveinal yellowing (tiger stripes)
• Small dark spots on fruit skin
• Sudden wilting (apoplexy)
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).prevention:
• Disinfect pruning tools
• Avoid pruning during wet weather
• Remove dead vines
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).treatment: No direct cure; focus on trunk surgery or vine replacement.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.description: Fungal infection causing irregular brown spots on leaves, leading to premature leaf fall.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.symptoms:
• Small, irregular reddish-brown spots
• Spots merge to form large dead areas
• Leaves drop prematurely
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.prevention:
• Destroy infected leaves
• Use resistant varieties
• Avoid dense foliage

Grape___healthy.description: Your grape vine appears to be in excellent health.
Grape___healthy.symptoms:
• Vibrant green leaves
• Firm fruit
• No visible spots or pests
Grape___healthy.prevention:
• Regular pruning
• Balanced irrigation
• Monitoring for pests

Maize___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_s.description: One of the most significant yield-limiting diseases of corn worldwide.
Maize___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_s.symptoms:
• Rectangular tan spots
• Spots parallel to leaf veins
• Grayish cast to lesions
Maize___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_s.prevention:
• Crop rotation
• Tillage to bury residue
• Hybrid selection
Maize___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_s.treatment: Apply foliar fungicides like Pyraclostrobin during silking.

Maize___Common_rust.description: Fungal disease causing small, brown pustules on leaf surfaces.
Maize___Common_rust.symptoms:
• Circular to elongate golden-brown pustules
• Pustules appear on both leaf surfaces
• Chlorosis and death of leaves in severe cases
Maize___Common_rust.prevention:
• Use resistant hybrids
• Early planting
• Avoid excessive nitrogen
Maize___Common_rust.treatment: Spray fungicides like Mancozeb if infection is high.

Maize___Northern_Leaf_Blight.description: Fungal disease that causes large, cigar-shaped lesions on leaves.
Maize___Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptoms:
• Long, elliptical, grayish-green lesions
• Lesions turn tan as they mature
• Reduced grain fill
Maize___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention:
• Tillage to reduce crop residue
• Crop rotation
• Use of resistant hybrids
Maize___Northern_Leaf_Blight.treatment: Timely application of triazole or strobilurin fungicides.

Maize___healthy.description: The maize plant shows no signs of disease or deficiency.
Maize___healthy.symptoms:
• Deep green, wide leaves
• Strong stalks
• No visible spots
Maize___healthy.prevention:
• Soil health management
• Adequate watering
• Regular scouting
Maize___healthy.treatment: Maintain current fertilizer and water schedules.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).description: A devastating bacterial disease spread by psyllids, affecting fruit quality.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).symptoms:
• Mottled yellow leaves (asymmetric yellowing)
• Small, lopsided fruit
• Bitter fruit juice
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).prevention:
• Control citrus psyllids
• Use disease-free nursery stock
• Remove infected trees
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).treatment: No known cure; management focuses on psyllid control and nutrition.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.description: Bacterial disease causing spots on leaves and fruit, leading to defoliation.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms:
• Small, water-soaked spots on leaves
• Spots turn brown and scabby
• Premature leaf drop
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention:
• Use pathogen-free seeds
• Avoid overhead irrigation
• Rotate crops
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based sprays regularly during wet periods.

Pepper,_bell___healthy.description: The bell pepper plant appears healthy and vigorous.
Pepper,_bell___healthy.symptoms:
• Glossy, dark green leaves
• Strong stems
• Healthy fruit set

Potato___Early_blight.description: Fungal disease characterized by 'target' spots with concentric rings.
Potato___Early_blight.symptoms:
• Dark brown spots with concentric rings
• Spots appear first on older leaves
• Yellowing around lesions
Potato___Early_blight.prevention:
• Use certified seeds
• Crop rotation with non-solanaceous crops
• Proper irrigation
Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Foliar sprays of Mancozeb or Chlorothalonil.

Potato___Late_blight.description: Highly destructive disease that can rapidly destroy potato fields in cool, wet weather.
Potato___Late_blight.symptoms:
• Large, irregular water-soaked lesions
• White fungal growth on leaf undersides
• Darkening and rotting of tubers
Potato___Late_blight.prevention:
• Destroy volunteer plants
• Use resistant varieties
• Good soil drainage

Potato___healthy.description: The potato plant is healthy and growing well.
Potato___healthy.symptoms:
• Lush green foliage
• No spots on leaves
• Healthy tuber development
Potato___healthy.prevention:
• Hill potatoes correctly
• Monitor for beetles
• Balanced N-P-K

Rice___Bacterial_Leaf_Blight.description: A highly destructive bacterial disease favored by high humidity and rain.
Rice___Bacterial_Leaf_Blight.symptoms:
• Yellow-orange stripes on leaf edges
• Wavy margins on lesions
• Milky bacterial ooze in morning
Rice___Bacterial_Leaf_Blight.prevention:
• Balanced nitrogen use
• Seed treatment with bleaching powder
• Proper drainage
Rice___Bacterial_Leaf_Blight.treatment: Spray Streptocycline (6g) mixed with Copper Oxychloride (500g) per acre.

Rice___Bakanae_Disease.description: A seed-borne fungal disease causing plants to grow abnormally tall and thin.
Rice___Bakanae_Disease.symptoms:
• Tall, spindly, pale yellow plants
• Poor root development
• Seedling death
Rice___Bakanae_Disease.prevention:
• Use certified disease-free seeds
• Seed treatment with fungicides
• Remove infected plants
Rice___Bakanae_Disease.treatment: Soak seeds in Carbendazim solution before sowing.

Rice___Blast.description: One of the most devastating rice diseases globally, affecting all growth stages.
Rice___Blast.symptoms:
• Diamond-shaped spots with gray centers
• Neck rot (breaking of panicles)
• Brown lesions on nodes
Rice___Blast.prevention:
• Avoid excessive Nitrogen
• Burn infected crop residue
• Use resistant varieties
Rice___Blast.treatment: Spray Tricyclazole 75 WP at 0.6g/liter of water.

Rice___Brown_Plant_Hopper.description: A pest that sucks sap, causing 'hopper burn' and spreading viruses.
Rice___Brown_Plant_Hopper.symptoms:
• Circular patches of drying plants
• Plants turn yellow then brown
• Visible insects at the base of stalks
Rice___Brown_Plant_Hopper.prevention:
• Alternate wetting and drying of fields
• Avoid dense planting
• Conserve natural enemies (spiders)
Rice___Brown_Plant_Hopper.treatment: Apply Pymetrozine or Dinotefuran at the base of the plants.

Rice___Brown_Spot.description: Fungal disease often associated with nutrient-deficient soil.
Rice___Brown_Spot.symptoms:
• Small oval brown spots
• Spots look like sesame seeds
• Reduced grain quality
Rice___Brown_Spot.prevention:
• Apply balanced fertilizers (NKP)
• Seed treatment with Carbendazim
• Improve soil health
Rice___Brown_Spot.treatment: Spray Mancozeb or Edifenphos if symptoms are widespread.

Rice___Chilo_Stem_Borer.description: The larvae bore into the rice stem, causing central shoot death.
Rice___Chilo_Stem_Borer.symptoms:
• 'Dead hearts' in early stages
• 'White heads' during flowering
• Holes in the stems
Rice___Chilo_Stem_Borer.prevention:
• Use pheromone traps
• Clip leaf tips before transplanting
• Conserve egg parasitoids
Rice___Chilo_Stem_Borer.treatment: Apply Cartap Hydrochloride or Fipronil granules.

Rice___False_Smut.description: Fungal disease that transforms individual grains into orange/black velvety balls.
Rice___False_Smut.symptoms:
• Large greenish-yellow balls on grains
• Balls turn orange then dark green/black
• Only a few grains per panicle affected
Rice___False_Smut.prevention:
• Use clean seeds
• Early planting
• Moderate nitrogen use
Rice___False_Smut.treatment: Spray Copper Oxychloride or Propiconazole at booting stage.

Rice___Hispa.description: A beetle whose larvae mine the leaf, leaving white parallel streaks.
Rice___Hispa.symptoms:
• Scraping of the upper leaf surface
• White parallel streaks on leaves
• Leaves become thin and dry
Rice___Hispa.prevention:
• Remove alternative weed hosts
• Avoid excessive nitrogen
• Clip leaf tips to remove eggs

Rice___Leaf_Scald.description: Fungal disease causing banding patterns on leaves, often starting from the tip.
Rice___Leaf_Scald.symptoms:
• Wavy-edged, water-soaked lesions
• Lesions turn light brown with dark bands
• Tips of leaves dry out
Rice___Leaf_Scald.prevention:
• Use disease-free seeds
• Balanced fertilization
• Good water management
Rice___Leaf_Scald.treatment: Spray fungicides like Benomyl or Carbendazim.

Rice___Narrow_Brown_Spot.description: Fungal disease appearing as short, narrow brown lesions on leaves.
Rice___Narrow_Brown_Spot.symptoms:
• Short, linear, reddish-brown lesions
• Lesions parallel to the veins
• Premature leaf senescence
Rice___Narrow_Brown_Spot.prevention:
• Plant resistant varieties
• Improve soil fertility
• Crop rotation
Rice___Narrow_Brown_Spot.treatment: Apply Propiconazole or Mancozeb if needed.

Rice___Nitrogen_Deficiency.description: Nutrient deficiency causing overall yellowing and poor growth.
Rice___Nitrogen_Deficiency.symptoms:
• Older leaves turn light green to yellow
• Stunted growth
• Thin stems and few tillers
Rice___Nitrogen_Deficiency.prevention:
• Soil testing
• Split application of Urea
• Use of Green Manures
Rice___Nitrogen_Deficiency.treatment: Top-dress with Urea or apply Neem-coated Urea.

Rice___Phosphorus_Deficiency.description: Lack of phosphorus causes dark green leaves and severely reduced tillering.
Rice___Phosphorus_Deficiency.symptoms:
• Stunted growth and dark green leaves
• Narrow, short, and erect leaves
• Poor root development and delayed flowering
Rice___Phosphorus_Deficiency.prevention:
• Apply basal dose of DAP or SSP
• Improve soil organic matter
• Maintain proper soil pH
Rice___Phosphorus_Deficiency.treatment: Apply soil or foliar phosphorus fertilizers.

Rice___Potassium_Deficiency.description: Deficiency causing yellowing and browning of leaf margins, increasing disease risk.
Rice___Potassium_Deficiency.symptoms:
• Yellowing and browning of leaf tips and edges
• Dark rusty spots on older leaves
• Weak stems and lodging
Rice___Potassium_Deficiency.prevention:
• Apply Muriate of Potash (MOP)
• Return rice straw to the field
• Balanced fertilizer application
Rice___Potassium_Deficiency.treatment: Apply Potash fertilizers during soil preparation.

Rice___Rice_Gall_Midge.description: Pest larvae cause the leaf sheath to form a hollow, silver-colored tube.
Rice___Rice_Gall_Midge.symptoms:
• Formation of 'Silver Shoots' (onion leaf galls)
• Stunted tillering
• Vigorous growth of side tillers
Rice___Rice_Gall_Midge.prevention:
• Early planting
• Use resistant varieties
• Remove weed hosts like wild rice

Rice___Rice_Leaf_Folder.description: Larvae fold the leaves and feed inside, creating white transparent streaks.
Rice___Rice_Leaf_Folder.symptoms:
• Folded leaves longitudinally
• White scraped patches on leaves
• Reduced photosynthetic area
Rice___Rice_Leaf_Folder.prevention:
• Avoid excessive use of Nitrogen
• Use of light traps
• Field sanitation

Rice___Rice_Skipper.description: Caterpillars that fold leaf blades together and feed on them.
Rice___Rice_Skipper.symptoms:
• Leaves webbed/folded into tubes
• Edges of leaves eaten away
• Smooth, green caterpillars found inside folds
Rice___Rice_Skipper.prevention:
• Manual collection of larvae
• Clean cultivation
• Natural predators (birds/wasps)
Rice___Rice_Skipper.treatment: Generally doesn't require chemicals; spray Quinalphos if severe.

Rice___Sheath_Blight.description: Fungal disease causing large oval spots on the leaf sheath near the water line.
Rice___Sheath_Blight.symptoms:
• Large, irregular, grayish-white spots on sheaths
• Spots have purple-brown borders
• Infection spreads to upper leaves
Rice___Sheath_Blight.prevention:
• Balanced fertilizer application
• Optimal plant spacing
• Weed control

Rice___Thrips.description: Tiny insects that suck sap from young leaves, causing curling and drying.
Rice___Thrips.symptoms:
• Silver streaks on leaves
• Leaves roll at the edges or tips
• Terminal parts of leaves dry up
Rice___Thrips.prevention:
• Flood the field for a few days
• Use resistant varieties
• Monitoring during dry weather

Rice___Tungro_Virus.description: Viral disease spread by Green Leafhoppers, causing severe stunting.
Rice___Tungro_Virus.symptoms:
• Severe stunting of plants
• Leaves turn yellow to orange-yellow
• Reduced number of tillers and sterile grains
Rice___Tungro_Virus.prevention:
• Control Green Leafhoppers (GLH)
• Plant resistant varieties
• Avoid planting during peak GLH seasons
Rice___Tungro_Virus.treatment: No cure for virus; use insecticides to control the vectors.

Rice___Yellow_Stem_Borer.description: A major pest where larvae destroy the growing point of the rice plant.
Rice___Yellow_Stem_Borer.symptoms:
• 'Dead hearts' (drying of central shoot)
• 'White heads' (empty panicles)
• Larvae found inside the stem
Rice___Yellow_Stem_Borer.prevention:
• Mass trapping with pheromones
• Harvest at ground level
• Destruction of stubble

Rice___healthy.description: The rice crop is in healthy condition.
Rice___healthy.symptoms:
• Strong tillering
• Uniform green leaves
• Healthy root system
Rice___healthy.prevention:
• Systematic weeding
• Proper water level
• Balanced nutrition

Soybean___healthy.description: The soybean crop is healthy and free from visible diseases.
Soybean___healthy.symptoms:
• Broad green leaves
• No visible spots or mottling
• Good pod development
Soybean___healthy.prevention:
• Seed inoculation
• Weed management
• Soil moisture monitoring

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.description: Bacterial disease causing small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves and stems.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.symptoms:
• Small, circular, dark spots on leaves
• Spots appear greasy on fruit
• Yellowing and eventual drop of leaves
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention:
• Use certified seeds
• Avoid overhead watering
• Remove crop debris

Tomato___Early_blight.description: Common fungal disease causing target-like spots on tomato leaves.
Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms:
• Concentric rings in brown spots
• Lower leaves affected first
• Stem lesions and fruit rot
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention:
• Rotate crops annually
• Mulch around plants
• Prune lower leaves

Tomato___Late_blight.description: Fungal-like organism that can destroy tomato plants quickly in wet weather.
Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms:
• Large, dark, water-soaked patches on leaves
• White fuzzy growth on undersides
• Rapid wilting and browning
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention:
• Use resistant cultivars
• Ensure good air circulation
• Monitor weather alerts

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.description: Fungal disease common in high humidity and poor ventilation.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.symptoms:
• Pale green spots on upper leaf surface
• Olive-green velvety mold on underside
• Leaves wither and drop
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention:
• Improve greenhouse ventilation
• Avoid wetting leaves
• Resistant varieties
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.treatment: Spray fungicides like Chlorothalonil or Difenoconazole.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.description: Fungal disease causing many small circular spots with gray centers.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms:
• Numerous small spots with dark borders
• Gray or tan centers in spots
• Leaves turn yellow and fall
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention:
• Control weeds
• Water at the base, not foliage
• Remove infected plants

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.description: Tiny pests that suck sap, causing stippling and webbing on leaves.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.symptoms:
• Tiny yellow/white spots (stippling)
• Fine webbing on leaf undersides
• Leaves look dusty and eventually dry up
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention:
• Increase humidity
• Maintain plant health
• Introduce predatory mites
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.treatment: Use Abamectin or neem oil sprays.

Tomato___Target_Spot.description: Fungal disease characterized by target-like lesions on leaves and fruit.
Tomato___Target_Spot.symptoms:
• Small, brown spots with concentric circles
• Spots expand and merge
• Pitted lesions on fruit
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention:
• Improve spacing for air flow
• Remove lower foliage
• Avoid high humidity

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.hindi_name: टमाटर का पीला पत्ता मरोड़ वायरस
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.description: A viral disease spread by whiteflies that can cause 100% crop loss.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms:
• Leaves curl upward and inward
• Intense yellowing of leaf edges
• Stunted plant growth
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention:
• Use whitefly-proof mesh
• Yellow sticky traps
• Remove silverleaf nightshade weeds
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.treatment: Control whiteflies using Neem oil or systemic insecticides.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.description: Viral disease that causes mottled patterns on leaves and reduces fruit quality.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms:
• Mottled light and dark green patterns
• Fern-like leaf growth
• Internal browning of fruit
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention:
• Do not smoke near plants
• Wash hands before handling
• Use certified virus-free seeds
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment: No cure for virus; remove and burn infected plants immediately.

Tomato___healthy.description: The tomato plant is in peak health.
Tomato___healthy.symptoms:
• Robust green growth
• No spots or wilting
• Healthy blossoms and fruit
Tomato___healthy.prevention:
• Consistent watering
• Staking/caging
• Monitoring for pests

not_a_leaf.description: The AI model did not detect a plant leaf in the photo.
not_a_leaf.symptoms:
• Blurred object
• Background noise
• Non-plant object
not_a_leaf.treatment: Please retake the photo focusing clearly on a single diseased leaf.
